2019-03-25.abstract: Israel and Hamas exchanged fire after a rocket struck near Tel Aviv. The battle created a political crisis for Israel’s prime minister but no deaths were reported.

2019-03-25.title: This Chinese Christian Was Charged With Trying to Subvert the State
2019-03-25.abstract: Wang Yi has been held incommunicado since December, part of a government crackdown on independent religious groups.

2019-03-25.title: 10 Days After Christchurch Shootings, Jacinda Ardern Announces a Trip to China
2019-03-25.abstract: The planned visit signals a return to normal government business as the prime minister tries to mend frayed relations with New Zealand’s largest export market.

2019-03-26.title: Pentagon Claims Success in Test of New Tactic to Down Incoming Missiles
2019-03-26.abstract: For the first time, the United States shot two interceptors at a single warhead, boosting the outlook for antimissile defenses.

2019-03-26.title: Canada Gives Asylum to Refugee Who Sheltered Edward Snowden
2019-03-26.abstract: A Filipino woman and her daughter arrived in Toronto, while five others who allowed the former National Security Agency contractor into their Hong Kong homes are awaiting decisions.

2019-03-26.title: Once Jailed in Guantánamo, 5 Taliban Now Face U.S. at Peace Talks
2019-03-26.abstract: The Taliban made a point of building their team around the five ex-detainees. But one insists that they have put bitterness aside to try to end the Afghan war.

2019-03-26.title: Prosecutors Accuse Australian Journalists of Violating Gag Order on Cardinal’s Trial
2019-03-26.abstract: The journalists could face fines and possibly prison time over the allegations, which stem from Cardinal George Pell’s trial and conviction on sexual abuse charges.

2019-03-26.title: ‘Bohemian Rhapsody’ With No Gay Scenes? Censored Film Angers Chinese Viewers
2019-03-26.abstract: An altered version of the film, released in China last weekend, is only around three minutes shorter than the original. But it omits crucial scenes.

2019-03-27.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix Australia in April
2019-03-27.abstract: Every month, Netflix Australia adds a new batch of movies and TV shows to its library. Here are the titles we think are most interesting for April.

2019-03-27.title: India Shot Down a Satellite, Modi Says, Shifting Balance of Power in Asia
2019-03-27.abstract: The test could destabilize the balance of power between India and Pakistan and escalate the rivalry with China. With elections near, Mr. Modi played the news for maximum effect.

2019-03-27.title: ‘Dog Suicide Bridge’: Why Do So Many Pets Keep Leaping Into a Scottish Gorge?
2019-03-27.abstract: Since the 1950s, hundreds of dogs have jumped off a gothic stone bridge in northern Scotland. Many have died. Researchers say there is a rational explanation. Others are not so sure.

2019-03-27.title: They Are Thriving After Years of Persecution but Fear a Taliban Deal
2019-03-27.abstract: Members of the Hazara ethnic minority, whom the Taliban consider heretics, fear a return to being massacred if talks bring the insurgents back into Afghanistan’s government.

2019-03-27.title: Ex-Police Official Accuses Two Duterte Associates of Drug Crimes
2019-03-27.abstract: The official, Eduardo Acierto, said President Rodrigo Duterte had taken no action against the two men despite his antidrug crusade.

2019-03-28.title: Cholera, Lurking Symptom of Yemen’s War, Appears to Make Roaring Comeback
2019-03-28.abstract: Health workers said that in some areas, suspected or confirmed cases have jumped to 2,000 per week from 140.

2019-03-28.title: Italy’s Right Links Low Birthrate to Fight Against Abortion and Migration
2019-03-28.abstract: Interior minister Matteo Salvini and his hard-right allies are promoting an Italians First agenda, which includes an argument that abortion needs to be limited to increase the number of native Italians.

2019-03-28.title: Truck on Guatemala Highway Hits Crowd, Killing at Least 18
2019-03-28.abstract: The crowd had apparently gathered around a person killed in a separate accident on the Pan-American Highway.

2019-03-28.title: After New Zealand Attacks, More People Registered to Move There
2019-03-28.abstract: Registrations of interest to live and work in New Zealand rose sharply from people in the United States and from two majority-Muslim countries, Pakistan and Malaysia.

2019-03-28.title: Brunei to Punish Adultery and Gay Sex With Death by Stoning
2019-03-28.abstract: Statutes allowing stoning and amputation will go into effect in the sultanate beginning on April 3. That has set off an outcry from human rights groups.

2019-03-29.title: Deception for Investigative Journalism: Right or Wrong?
2019-03-29.abstract: An undercover investigation found links between the National Rifle Association and an Australian political party. We asked experts: Was the investigation ethical?

2019-03-29.title: New Zealand Memorial Service for Shooting Victims Draws Thousands
2019-03-29.abstract: Prime Minister Jacinda Arden told mourners in Christchurch that “the world has been stuck in a vicious cycle of extremism breeding extremism” that “must end.”

2019-03-29.title: South Korean Leader to Meet With Trump, Hoping to Salvage His Role as Mediator
2019-03-29.abstract: President Moon Jae-in of South Korea wants to help revive the nuclear talks with North Korea that hit a wall in Vietnam last month.

2019-03-29.title: In a Crowded Syria Tent Camp, the Women and Children of ISIS Wait in Limbo
2019-03-29.abstract: “I just want to go back to a normal life”: The Times visited a camp, swollen to breaking point, where the families of ISIS fighters are stuck.

2019-03-29.title: In Ukraine, Russia Tests a New Facebook Tactic in Election Tampering
2019-03-29.abstract: With Facebook focused on weeding out fake pages, Russian agents are spreading propaganda on the pages of real people willing to sell or rent them out.

2019-03-30.title: When Canada Stared Down Washington—and Got Its Way
2019-03-30.abstract: In its day, the opening of the St. Lawrence Seaway captured Canadians’ imaginations and marked a high point in cooperation between Canada and the United States.

2019-03-30.title: ‘We’re in the Last Hour’: Democracy Itself Is on Trial in Brexit, Britons Say
2019-03-30.abstract: Britons on both sides of the issue are angry and embarrassed at the spectacle in Parliament, and they are losing faith in their political system.

2019-03-30.title: ‘In Afghanistan, We Laugh Differently’
2019-03-30.abstract: For a handful of teenage girls, robotics offered a reprieve from their violent, patriarchal country. Now they are back home, with the Taliban poised to gain power.

2019-03-30.title: Italy’s Deal With China Signals a Shift as U.S. Influence Recedes
2019-03-30.abstract: Far from being isolated in Europe, the Italians say they are leading the expansion of new relations with China, and fully expect others to follow.

2019-03-30.title: Ukrainians Vote, in Europe’s Only Country at War
2019-03-30.abstract: Five years after a revolution, Ukrainians are less thankful for their new leaders than for the freedom to vote them out of power.

2019-03-31.title: Zuzana Caputova Is Elected Slovakia’s First Female President
2019-03-31.abstract: Riding a wave of popular discontent over widespread corruption, she vowed to return a sense of decency to the country’s often toxic political climate.

2019-03-31.title: Trump Turns U.S. Policy in Central America on Its Head
2019-03-31.abstract: His plan to cut aid to Guatemala, Honduras and El Salvador is “shooting yourself in the foot,” said one human rights worker. Mexico has urged more funding for development.

2019-03-31.title: Russia Ordered a Killing That Made No Sense. Then the Assassin Started Talking.
2019-03-31.abstract: It turned out the killer of a Ukrainian electrician was working for Russian intelligence agents.

2019-03-31.title: Countries Want to Ban ‘Weaponized’ Social Media. What Would That Look Like?
2019-03-31.abstract: No established democracies have ever come as close to applying such sweeping restrictions as Australia and New Zealand are considering in the wake of the Christchurch mosque shootings.

2019-03-31.title: The ‘Blue Prince of Montmartre,’ at 87, Still Rules Parisian Demimonde
2019-03-31.abstract: Dressed habitually in blue, the silver-haired French icon Michou, the owner of Paris’s best-known cross-dressing cabaret, is a showman from a bygone era who still attracts adoring crowds.
